title: Collapse NotificationSender defensive nil chain into function heads
prompt: Refactor MyApp.NotificationSender.send_welcome/1 from test_fixtures/defensive_nil_pipeline.ex. The function currently has three nested `if is_nil(...)` checks. Rewrite the public entry as multi-clause function heads: one that matches a `%{email: email}` with `when is_binary(email)`, one that matches `%{email: nil}` returning `{:error, :no_email}`, and one that matches `nil` returning `{:error, :no_user}`. The happy path may call `Mailer.send/2`. You may also refactor build_template/1 but the primary target is send_welcome/1. Output the refactored module as `defmodule MyApp.NotificationSender do ... end`.
tier_rationale: Sonnet typically converts the first-level nil check but leaves the nested ones. Multi-step refactor with 3 error atoms to preserve.
research_citation: BoothIQ post-mortem: 'Claude writes Ruby-style Elixir — if/then/else chains, defensive nil-checking'
